key_insight: BatteryMat combines fast machine-learning screening with DFT validation to rank lithium-ion cathode candidates by average voltage while preserving thermodynamic consistency.
engineering_relevance: The framework can accelerate cathode discovery across large materials databases by narrowing millions of structures to a small set of DFT-ready leads.
method: Tier 1: ALIGNN predicts average voltage as a rapid surrogate screen over JARVIS-DFT and Alexandria structures.
Tier 2: ALIGNN-FF delithiation profiles validate survivors using force-field-based voltage traces.
Tier 3: automated supercell DFT with PBE+U or optB88-vdW+U finalizes voltage and capacity estimates, with the functional chosen by spacegroup.
evidence_signal: ALIGNN-FF training on 7,610 delithiation voltages achieved MAE 0.17 V and R² = 0.94, and the DFT tier matched four commercial cathodes within about 0.3 V of experimental average voltage.
watch_item: The ML model is only distilling the force-field protocol and the pipeline prioritizes existing structures, so surrogate hits still require careful DFT validation before being treated as confirmed cathodes.
paper_title: BatteryMat: a hierarchical machine-learning and DFT framework for average-voltage screening of lithium-ion cathode materials
